input.evidence_excerpt: /// Train on one labeled example. label in {0.0, 1.0}. pub fn train(&mut self, x: &[f32], label: f32) -> f32 { let n = self.n_inputs.min(x.len()); let p = self.predict(x); let err = p - label; // gradient of binary cross-entropy after sigmoid let loss = -(label * (p + 1e-8).ln() + (1.0 - label) * (1.0 - p + 1e-8).ln()); for i in 0..n { self.weights[i] -= self.lr * err * x[i]; } self.bias -= self.lr * err; self.n_train += 1; if (p > 0.5 && label > 0.5) || (p <= 0.5 && label <= 0.5) { self.n_correct += 1; } self.last_loss = loss; loss }

input.evidence_excerpt: fn levenshtein(a: &str, b: &str) -> usize { let m = a.len(); let n = b.len(); if m == 0 { return n; } if n == 0 { return m; } let a_chars: Vec<char> = a.chars().collect(); let b_chars: Vec<char> = b.chars().collect(); let mut prev = vec![0usize; n + 1]; let mut curr = vec![0usize; n + 1]; for j in 0..=n { prev[j] = j; } for i in 1..=m { curr[0] = i; for j in 1..=n { let cost = if a_chars[i - 1] == b_chars[j - 1] { 0 } else { 1 }; curr[j] = (prev[j] + 1).min(curr[j - 1] + 1).min(prev[j - 1] + cost); } std::mem::swap(&mut prev, &mut curr); } prev[n] }
